headline.name: General Sans
headline.license: Fontshare — free for personal and commercial use
headline.rationale: General Sans is a geometric sans-serif with sharp, confident letterforms and clean terminals. It has the authority of a neo-grotesque but with slightly warmer geometry that avoids feeling sterile. Its wide character set and variable font support make it versatile across headline weights. Aligns with the Palantir/Vercel/Linear aesthetic referenced in the strategy brief.

body.name: Inter
body.rationale: Inter was designed specifically for computer screens with excellent legibility at small sizes. Its tall x-height, open apertures, and tabular figure support make it ideal for both running text and UI contexts. Near-universal browser support and variable font availability ensure performance. This is the same typeface used by Linear, Vercel, and other enterprise SaaS brands in Parq's aesthetic territory.

data.name: JetBrains Mono
data.rationale: JetBrains Mono has increased letter height for improved readability, distinct letterforms that prevent character confusion in data contexts (1/l/I, 0/O), and a technical precision that reinforces Parq's AI credibility. Used for metrics, dashboard callouts, detection accuracy figures, and any numerical data displays. Its geometric construction pairs naturally with General Sans headlines.

display.usage: Hero headlines on landing pages. One per page maximum.

h1.usage: Primary page titles. Section heroes on marketing pages.

h2.usage: Major section headings. Pitch deck slide titles.

h3.usage: Subsection headings. Card titles. Feature names.

h4.usage: Tertiary headings. Dialog titles. Sidebar section headers.

h5.usage: Minor headings. List group headers. Eyebrow text (when uppercased).

body_large.usage: Lead paragraphs. Introductory text. High-emphasis body copy.

body.usage: Default running text. Descriptions. Form labels.

body_small.usage: Secondary text. Table content. Helper text.

caption.usage: Image captions. Footnotes. Timestamps. Legal text.

overline.usage: Section labels. Category tags. Eyebrow text above headings.

data_label.usage: Metric labels, dashboard values, code-style annotations, inline data.

font_weights.strategy: Use weight variation to establish hierarchy. Limit to 3 weights per typeface to maintain visual consistency and minimize font loading.

semibold.usage: Display, H1, H2 — primary headlines that need maximum presence

medium.usage: H3, H4, H5 — secondary headings that support hierarchy without competing with primary headlines

regular.usage: Running text, descriptions, all default body content

medium.usage: Emphasis within body text, overlines, labels, navigation items, buttons

semibold.usage: Strong emphasis only — use sparingly. Bold CTAs, key stats in running text.

regular.usage: Data labels, inline code, small metric annotations

medium.usage: Hero statistics, large metric displays, dashboard primary values

loading_strategy.method: Variable fonts where available; subset to Latin character set
loading_strategy.note: Variable fonts load all weights in a single file, eliminating multiple HTTP requests. Preload the two most critical typefaces. JetBrains Mono can load asynchronously since data displays are typically below the fold.

responsive_typography.strategy: Fluid typography using CSS clamp() for headlines. Fixed sizes for body text with breakpoint-based adjustments. Headlines scale significantly; body text scales modestly to maintain readability.

wide.viewport_label: Large monitors — cap maximum sizes here

body.note: Body text remains fixed at 16px across all breakpoints. This is the minimum comfortable reading size on all devices.

minimum_sizes.body_text: 16px — never go below this for running text
minimum_sizes.caption_text: 11px — absolute floor for any visible text
minimum_sizes.touch_target_label: 14px — minimum for text within interactive elements on mobile

line_length.note: Applies to running body text. Headlines and short-form text can span wider.

paragraph_spacing.after_heading: 0.75em — tighter coupling between heading and its content
paragraph_spacing.before_heading: 2em — clear visual break before new sections

heading_to_body_ratio.display_to_body: 3.81:1 — strong visual anchor for hero sections
heading_to_body_ratio.assessment: The Major Third scale produces moderate, professional ratios. The display and h1 levels create strong anchors for marketing pages, while h3-h5 levels create subtle but clear hierarchy for content-dense UI. This matches the 'confident but restrained' personality — authority without shouting.

contrast_and_color.body_text: Minimum 7:1 contrast ratio against background (WCAG AAA)
contrast_and_color.heading_text: Minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA for large text)
contrast_and_color.caption_text: Minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio despite smaller size
contrast_and_color.note: Specific color values to be determined by color system. These are minimum ratios to maintain.

use_for: The 'Parq' portion of the logo wordmark
never_for: Running body text longer than 2 lines

use_for: Pitch deck body text and bullet points
The 'AI' portion of the logo wordmark (reinforces technical identity)

hierarchy_rules: Every page must have exactly one h1. Use display size only for hero sections on primary landing pages.
Maintain strict heading order: never skip from h2 to h4. Visual hierarchy and semantic hierarchy must align.
Use weight (medium vs semibold) rather than size to create emphasis within the same heading level.
Overline labels (small, uppercase, tracked-out) should precede h2 or h3 headings to provide category context without adding a heading level.
Data callouts use JetBrains Mono at data_display size with a body-weight label beneath in Inter — this creates a visual pattern that instantly signals 'metric' throughout the brand.
Never use italic for emphasis in headlines. Use weight change or the accent color instead.
Limit underlines to hyperlinks only. Do not use text-decoration for emphasis.

pitch_deck_specifics.slide_titles: General Sans Semibold, 36-44pt equivalent depending on slide type
pitch_deck_specifics.slide_body: Inter Regular, 18-22pt, max 5 bullet points per slide
pitch_deck_specifics.key_metrics: JetBrains Mono Medium, 60-80pt for the number, Inter Regular 16pt for the label beneath
pitch_deck_specifics.speaker_notes: Inter Regular 14pt — notes are for reference, not projection
general_rules: Maximum 6 lines of text per slide for readability at distance
Use the data_display pattern (large mono number + small label) for all statistical proof points
Slide headlines should be sentences, not topics — 'Parq reduces enforcement costs by 90%' not 'Cost Reduction'

website_specifics.hero_sections: Display size (fluid) with tight letter-spacing. Max 8-10 words for the headline. Lead paragraph in body_large.
website_specifics.feature_sections: H2 for section title. H4 or H5 for individual feature names. Body for descriptions. Keep descriptions under 3 lines.
website_specifics.social_proof: Data display size for the metric in JetBrains Mono. Body_small in Inter for the description beneath.
website_specifics.navigation: Inter Medium at body_small size (14px). All caps optional for top nav, sentence case for mobile nav.
website_specifics.footer: Inter Regular at body_small (14px). Caption size (12px) for copyright and legal links.

font_face_declarations: @font-face { font-family: 'General Sans'; src: url('/fonts/GeneralSans-Variable.woff2') format('woff2-variations'); font-weight: 400 700; font-display: swap; }
@font-face { font-family: 'Inter'; src: url('/fonts/Inter-Variable.woff2') format('woff2-variations'); font-weight: 400 700; font-display: swap; }
@font-face { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono'; src: url('/fonts/JetBrainsMono-Variable.woff2') format('woff2-variations'); font-weight: 400 700; font-display: swap; }
